paper_title: Comparison of RSL Data and Modeled Sea-Level Curves in the Aegean

methods.description: Comparison of field-based relative sea-level (RSL) curves from various locations in the Aegean Sea to understand spatial variability in RSL changes due to tectonic activity, sediment compaction, and other factors.

steps.description: Collect RSL data from various studies conducted in the Aegean Sea region.
steps.searchable_summary: gather RSL data from studies by Syrides et al., Pavlopoulos et al., and Seeliger et al.
inputs: RSL data from Syrides et al. (2009)
RSL data from Pavlopoulos et al. (2007, 2013)
RSL data from Seeliger et al. (2017)

steps.description: Analyze the collected RSL data to identify trends and correlations with the study's RSL curve.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze RSL data for trends and correlations with study curve
outputs: Analysis report on RSL trends and correlations

steps.description: Generate maps and figures to visualize the spatial variability in RSL changes across the Aegean region.
steps.searchable_summary: create visualizations of RSL spatial variability
inputs: Analysis report on RSL trends and correlations
outputs: Maps and figures of RSL changes

methods.description: Comparison of modeled sea-level curves with field-based data to assess discrepancies and understand the impact of subsidence and other factors on RSL predictions.

steps.description: Calculate two models of RSL rise for the study area using ICE-6G (VM5a) and ANU models.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate RSL rise models using ICE-6G and ANU

steps.description: Compare modeled RSL curves with field-based data to identify offsets and discrepancies.
steps.searchable_summary: compare modeled RSL curves with field data

steps.description: Calculate subsidence rates using historical data and compare with modeled predictions.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and compare subsidence rates with models
